patches_actually_applied.material_change_justification: Coral status upgraded to Critical based on peer-reviewed confirmation of bleaching recurrence shorter than recovery timescale; AMOC proxy value updated with new Science Advances projection

new_value.chain: El Niño drought reduces rainfall → crop failure and food insecurity → internal displacement and northward migration → political instability and governance pressure
El Niño drier conditions reduce Mekong flow → rice and hydropower disruption → transboundary resource competition → food insecurity in import-dependent populations
El Niño drought reduces agricultural productivity → food insecurity in Venezuela → displacement into Colombia → cross-border tensions and governance strain
El Niño SST anomalies trigger coral bleaching → fisheries collapse and coastal protection loss → food insecurity and permanent displacement → no viable return pathway
AMOC slowdown weakens monsoon rainfall → food and water stress → internal displacement and cross-border migration → conflict risk in fragile governance zones

patches_actually_applied.material_change_justification: All four El Niño-driven cascades moved to Active status based on C3S seasonal forecast confirmation; Sahel AMOC cascade remains Monitoring pending monsoon season data

new_value.key_indicator: Global surface air temperature 14.89°C (April 2026), 0.52°C above 1991-2020 average; extra-polar SST second highest on record
new_value.week_development: C3S confirms April 2026 joint third-warmest April globally and second-highest extra-polar ocean SST on record, continuing trajectory from 2023-2025 three-year average exceeding 1.5°C above pre-industrial for first time. Reasoner analysis confirms structural acceleration with 2034 breach projection nine years ahead of 2015 baseline.
new_value.asymmetric: Sustained SST anomaly during El Niño development represents compounding radiative forcing and ocean heat content trajectory that reshapes physical risk baseline for all coastal and marine-dependent systems

patches_actually_applied.material_change_justification: April 2026 SST data and 2034 breach projection represent material update to climate change boundary trajectory assessment

new_value.this_week_development: Communications Earth and Environment study quantifies IMO 2020 sulfur cuts reduced aerosol cooling over Great Barrier Reef by 11 watts per square meter, contributing 5 to 10 percent additional GBR thermal stress
new_value.key_metric: Regional aerosol decrease produces ecological harm with no international liability framework

patches_actually_applied.material_change_justification: First peer-reviewed quantification of IMO 2020 aerosol-coral thermal stress pathway represents material development in attribution gap case
